title.en: Government Pension Delayed? Interest Rights, Grievance Ladders and CAT/Court Remedies

answerBox.en: Pension is deferred salary — a right, not a bounty (Supreme Court, D.S. Nakara). Delays typically trace to incomplete papers, unresolved audit objections, or files shuttling between the department, PAO and bank. The escalation that works: written follow-up with the Head of Office, CPENGRAMS grievance, appeal to the department's pension nodal officer — and interest on delayed gratuity/pension arrears is payable under the CCS Pension Rules when delay is administrative.

en.title: Locate where the file actually is
en.summary: HOO → PAO → CPAO → bank: four stations, one stuck file.
en.detail: The pipeline: your **Head of Office** compiles papers (service book, no-dues, Form 5/7), the **Pay & Accounts Office** issues the **PPO** (Pension Payment Order), CPAO (central)/treasury routes it, and the **pension-disbursing bank** starts payment. A written status request to the HOO + an **[RTI](/help/government-paperwork/rti-application-how-to)** asking "which station holds the file, since when, and why" identifies the choke point in two weeks — you cannot push a file you cannot locate.

en.title: Invoke provisional pension — it exists for this
en.summary: 100% provisional pension + provisional gratuity while papers pend.
en.detail: Where finalisation is delayed (audit objections, disciplinary shadows, paperwork), the rules (CCS Pension Rules — Rule 62/64 framework) mandate **provisional pension (100% of the entitlement)** and provisional gratuity — you are not meant to starve while offices reconcile. Demand it in writing citing the rule; the same applies in most state rule-sets. Family pension claims follow parallel provisional protections. Retirees under NPS/UPS have their own annuity/assured-payout timelines — the grievance machinery below covers them too.

en.title: The grievance ladder that moves files
en.detail: File on **[CPENGRAMS](https://pensionersportal.gov.in)** (the pension-specific grievance system — DOPPW monitors disposal) with your PPO/file references; escalate within the portal and to the department's **pension nodal officer** and the **Directorate of Pension** (states). **Pension Adalats** (periodic, department-wise) resolve chronic cases on the spot — get yours listed. Banks sitting on started-but-stopped payments answer through the bank's grievance cell + the [RBI Ombudsman](/help/consumer-complaints/bank-charges-wrongly-deducted). Every step in writing; oral follow-ups produce sympathy, not PPOs.

en.title: Interest — and the tribunal/court lane
en.summary: Delayed dues carry interest; CAT/HC orders both fast.
en.detail: Administrative delay in gratuity/pension attracts **interest** (CCS Pension Rules provide it on delayed gratuity; courts extend interest on arrears where delay is the department's — a consistent line since *D.S. Nakara* framed pension as a right). Stonewalled beyond months: central employees move the **Central Administrative Tribunal** (states: their tribunals/High Court writs) — pension matters are the tribunals' bread and butter, with directions + interest + costs routine. An advocate's **₹999 notice** to the department citing the rule-based interest liability often produces the PPO without the petition.

en.footnote: Start the paper chase a year before retirement — the rules schedule preparation then, and files completed pre-retirement rarely become this page's problem.

en: pension nahi aa rahi kya kare

en.q: My PPO is stuck for months after retirement. What single step helps most?
en.a: The RTI asking which office holds the file, since when, and the reason — paired with a CPENGRAMS grievance quoting the answer. Named-station accountability moves pension files better than any visit; add the provisional-pension demand so money flows meanwhile.

en.q: What is provisional pension and who sanctions it?
en.a: Where final pension cannot be sanctioned by retirement (paperwork/audit/disciplinary pendency), the Head of Office must sanction provisional pension — now at 100% of the assessed entitlement — and provisional gratuity, adjustable on finalisation. It is a duty, not a favour: demand it in writing citing the rule.

en.q: Am I entitled to interest for the delay?
en.a: Delayed gratuity carries statutory interest under the CCS framework when delay is administrative; for pension arrears, tribunals and courts award interest where the department caused the delay. Claim it expressly in your representations — silence waives what the rules would give.

en.q: Family pension after the pensioner's death is stuck. Same process?
en.a: Yes with additions: death certificate, joint-photo PPO/nomination, and the family-pension conversion happen at the bank + PAO. The provisional protections and CPENGRAMS ladder apply equally; banks must not stop payment pending life-certificate technicalities where succession is clear. Escalate bank delays to the RBI Ombudsman.

en.q: Disciplinary proceedings were pending when I retired. Can they hold everything?
en.a: They can withhold/withdraw pension only per the rules (with President/Governor-level sanction in serious cases) — but provisional pension is still payable during pendency, and gratuity withholding has defined limits. Long-stalled proceedings themselves are challengeable at CAT. Get rule-specific advice; blanket "everything is frozen" positions rarely survive scrutiny.

reviewer: Adv. Arjun Nair, Bar Council of Kerala, KER/0973/2012
author: WakilBhai Editorial Team
